title: Some customer phone numbers are wrong in TaskFlow
body: We noticed that some of our customer contact records have incorrect phone numbers. The numbers look truncated or mangled. Our support team caught it because they tried calling a few customers and got wrong numbers. We have about 2,000 customer records and I'd estimate maybe 50-100 have wrong numbers. This is a serious data integrity issue for us.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — data integrity issues like this are definitely worth investigating carefully.

To help us understand the pattern of corruption: could you share 2-3 examples of what the incorrect phone numbers look like compared to what they should be? For instance, are digits missing from the beginning, the end, or somewhere in the middle? And do you know roughly when these numbers were entered or last updated — were they recent entries, or have they been in the system for a while?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
Sure, let me pull up a couple examples. One customer is based in Ireland and their number should be +353 1 234 5678 but in TaskFlow it shows as +35 1 234 5678 — so it looks like the country code got cut short. Another one is a customer in Antigua, their number starts with +1268 but it's showing as +12 something. Meanwhile our US and UK customers seem fine, their numbers look correct.

As for timing, I think this started maybe 2 weeks ago? I'm not 100% sure of the exact date but our support team started noticing wrong numbers around then. These aren't new records — they've been in the system for months, so something must have changed them.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thank you for those examples — they're very helpful. The pattern is striking: it looks like country codes longer than 2 digits are being truncated, which would explain why US (+1) and UK (+44) numbers are unaffected but Ireland (+353) and Antigua (+1268) are.

To help us narrow down the cause: do you know if anything changed in your TaskFlow setup around 2 weeks ago? For example, a software update, a data import/export, a change to phone number formatting settings, or any integrations that sync contact data? Also, is this still happening — if you edit a customer's phone number back to the correct value now, does it stay correct or does it get mangled again?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
We do have a nightly sync that imports customer data from our Salesforce CRM into TaskFlow — that's been running for months though. I'm not sure if there was a TaskFlow update around that time, I'd have to check with our dev team. I don't manage the software updates myself.

As for editing numbers back — honestly I haven't tried that yet because there are so many affected records. But now that you mention it, if the nightly sync is overwriting them, fixing them manually might just get undone the next night. That's a scary thought. I'll try correcting one and see if it sticks after tonight's sync.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: That's a great instinct about the nightly sync — it's very likely the key piece here. Could you tell me a bit more about how that sync works? Specifically: does it use an API integration, a CSV/file import, or something else? And if possible, could you check the sync logs from around 2 weeks ago to see if anything changed — for example, a new field mapping, a format change, or any errors? That would help us figure out whether the truncation is happening during the import process itself.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm not super technical on how the sync is set up — I know it pulls data from Salesforce automatically every night. I think it uses some kind of API connection but I couldn't tell you the specifics. Our dev team set it up. I don't have access to the sync logs myself, but I can ask them to look. I do remember someone mentioning that a developer made a change to how phone numbers are formatted or normalized a couple weeks back, but I'm not sure of the details. I'll ask the team to check the logs and any recent changes to the import process and get back to you.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — having the dev team check the sync logs and that recent formatting change will likely pinpoint the root cause.

In the meantime, one thing you could check right now that would save a lot of investigation time: can you look at those same customers (the Ireland and Antigua ones) directly in Salesforce and confirm their phone numbers are still correct there? If the numbers are fine in Salesforce but wrong in TaskFlow, that confirms the corruption is happening during the import. If they're also wrong in Salesforce, we'd be looking at a different source for the problem entirely.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Good idea, I just checked Salesforce and the numbers look correct there. The Ireland customer still shows +353 1 234 5678 and the Antigua one has the full +1268 number. So yeah, it's definitely something happening when the data comes into TaskFlow. I'll follow up with the dev team about those logs and the phone number formatting change.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Phone numbers with 3+ digit country codes truncated to 2 digits during Salesforce nightly sync","problem":"Approximately 50-100 of ~2,000 customer phone number records in TaskFlow have corrupted country codes. Numbers with country codes longer than 2 digits (e.g., Ireland +353, Antigua +1268) are being truncated to 2 digits (+35, +12), while 1-2 digit country codes (US +1, UK +44) are unaffected. The corruption originated in the nightly Salesforce-to-TaskFlow sync and the source data in Salesforce remains correct.","root_cause_hypothesis":"A code change made approximately 2 weeks ago to phone number formatting or normalization logic in the Salesforce sync pipeline is truncating the country code portion of international phone numbers to a maximum of 2 digits. This is likely a regex or substring operation that assumes all country codes are 1-2 digits, or a field-width constraint applied during parsing.","reproduction_steps":["Identify a customer record in Salesforce with a country code of 3+ digits (e.g., +353 for Ireland)","Confirm the phone number is correct and complete in Salesforce","Wait for or manually trigger the nightly Salesforce-to-TaskFlow sync","Check the same customer record in TaskFlow — the country code should appear truncated to 2 digits"],"environment":"TaskFlow application with nightly API-based data sync from Salesforce CRM. Approximately 2,000 customer records. Issue began approximately 2 weeks ago, coinciding with a developer change to phone number formatting/normalization logic.","severity":"high","impact":"Support team cannot reach affected customers by phone. Data corruption is ongoing — the nightly sync likely re-corrupts any manually corrected numbers. Affects international customers with 3+ digit country codes (estimated 50-100 records).","recommended_fix":"1. Review git history from ~2 weeks ago for changes to phone number formatting, normalization, or parsing in the Salesforce sync code. Look for regex patterns, substring operations, or field-width constraints on the country code portion. 2. Fix the formatting logic to preserve full country codes of any length (1-4 digits per E.164). 3. After deploying the fix, re-run the sync (or a targeted backfill) to restore correct numbers from Salesforce. 4. Consider adding a validation check that flags phone numbers whose country code doesn't match a known valid code.","proposed_test_case":"Create test records with phone numbers using country codes of varying lengths (1 digit: +1, 2 digits: +44, 3 digits: +353, 4 digits: +1268) and run them through the sync/normalization pipeline. Assert that all country codes are preserved in full after processing.","information_gaps":["Exact date and nature of the phone number formatting code change ~2 weeks ago","Whether manually correcting a number in TaskFlow survives the next nightly sync (reporter testing this)","Sync logs from the period when corruption began","Whether the issue affects only the sync path or also manual phone number entry in TaskFlow"]}
